summary:
I found the issue! The `incrementCounter` function in `app/counter.js` was using a non-atomic "read-modify-write" pattern (`get` followed by `set`) to update the counter in Redis. This caused a race condition when multiple requests hit the server concurrently, leading to lost increments.

I have updated the code to use Redis's atomic `INCR` command instead, ensuring that all concurrent increments are recorded accurately. I then ran `./test.sh` and it now correctly reports 100 out of 100 increments passing.
